items.title: Famous outback race claims another life after serious motorbike crash

children.content: A motorbike rider has died in the Central Australian outback after a Finke Desert Race crash, with the two-day event expected to continue as police investigate.

image.altText: A row of motorbikes at the starting line, their tyres are kicking up clumps of the dirt track.

items.title: Remote community love story told through first-time director's rom-com

children.content: In a First Nations romantic comedy debuting in Sydney this week, a new writer-director has used her lived experience to tell the story of young love in a remote Northern Territory community.

image.altText: A young Indigenous woman looks over her left shoulder towards a shirtless man standing behind her.

items.title: Marilyn's world 'fell apart' with one post. What happened next helped her heal

children.content: Marilyn was just five when she first encountered grief. Now 18, the teenager says she's been to more funerals than birthday parties.

gallery.altText: A young woman with a flower tucked behind her ear smiles as she holds onto her necklace.

thumbnail.altText: A young woman with a flower tucked behind her ear smiles as she holds onto her necklace.

items.title: 'Girl who screams most nights': NT hearings address child protection system

children.content: Some foster carers have voiced support for the NT's proposed changes to child protection laws, but others say the current legislation already outlines individual safety as the paramount consideration.

image.altText: Nine people sitting along a long table, each with microphones before them, facing another table where politicians are sitting.

items.title: Country's biggest barra fishing comp under way, with some reporting more crocs

children.content: The country's biggest annual barramundi fishing competition is under way on the Northern Territory's remote Daly River, after the tournament was delayed by six weeks due to extensive flooding.

image.altText: White man standing on boat, wearing long sleeve purple / navy hoodie, holding barramundi fish from belly and mouth, posing

items.title: Mongols bikie escapes custody after scaling court dock, dashing past security

children.content: A manhunt is continuing in Darwin this weekend for a 44-year-old bikie after he scaled an enclosed courthouse dock and escaped from custody.

image.altText: The outside of the ABC Darwin building, looking out towards the Darwin Local Court.

gallery.altText: A man in a green top running across a road, away from a court building, in a CBD.

thumbnail.altText: A man in a green top running across a road, away from a court building, in a CBD.

items.title: Kumanjayi Little Baby's family were forced to leave home community, MP says

children.content: Member for Lingiari Marion Scrymgour says the mother of Kumanjayi Little Baby had been living in insecure housing in Alice Springs so she could access specialist support for her daughter, before the five-year-old's tragic death in April.

image.altText: Two women sit opposite each other.

items.title: 'Like opening an oven': NT constable praised for 'heroic' rescue attempt

children.content: A "heroic" Alice Springs police officer who risked his life repeatedly entering a burning building in trying to rescue a three-year-old boy will be awarded a medal of valour.

image.altText: A shirt memorialising Mitchell Thomas that reads In loving memory of Mitchell

items.title: Couple back at Finke Desert Race after finding love via social post

children.content: When motorbike enthusiast Ben Rawcliffe posted on a Finke Desert Race Facebook group looking for his future wife, his post garnered hundreds of comments. Now, he's back in the red centre, supporting his new partner as she takes to the tough off-road track.

articles.title: Finke Desert Race legends reflect on event ahead of 50th anniversary

children.content: The 460-kilometre test of skill and endurance through the central Australian outback has become one of the most famous off-road races in the world.

articles.title: Hollywood legend set to bring Finke Desert Race to the big screen

children.content: The gruelling outback Finke Desert Race is set for Hollywood treatment after Marvel's Chris Hemsworth backs a feature-length movie.

image.altText: Chris Hemsworth stares into the camera.

thumbnail.altText: A man and a woman in motorbike fear in front of a range with the sun behind.

items.title: Inside a Trump-linked plan to turn a communist-era military base into a luxe resort

children.content: Ivanka Trump has floated "manifestations" of luxury holidays. Meanwhile, Albanians are claiming "irreversible" ecological damage. Why protests are shaking Albania.

Concept art of rooms jutting out of a hill.

items.title: Blues hold on for victory over Bombers at the MCG

children.content: Carlton continues its resurgence under interim coach Josh Fraser with a five-point victory over Essendon, after Sydney completes a thrilling victory over St Kilda. Follow live.

items.title: 'Embarrassing' Tigers humiliated by Penrith, Sharks down Dragons

children.content: The Penrith Panthers thrash Wests 68-0 to record the biggest win in club history, as Cronulla notches a 34-12 victory over St George Illawarra at Shark Park.

image.altText: An NRL player in white and black and orange has his hand over his mouth in afternoon sun

items.title: SA Police investigating after man dies after 'ineffective' taser arrest

children.content: Police are investigating an incident at Clare, in South Australia's mid north, where a man became unresponsive during an arrest.

image.altText: multiple officers on the street

items.title: 'Beyond our capacity' to defend sea trade lanes, Shoebridge says

children.content: Greens defence spokesman David Shoebridge says it is "ridiculous" to think Australia's military could defend the nation's global trade lanes, and acquiring nuclear-powered submarines under AUKUS only invites Australia to a conflict between the United States and China.

image.altText: David Shoebridge wears blue suit jacket, pink shirt and no tie. Speaking in a corridor to the media.

items.title: How Tasmania's planned new stadium could trigger deal for Hobart Hurricanes

children.content: Cricket's smallest state says it could become a major player in privatisation, with a potential Hobart Hurricanes deal tied to Tasmania's planned Macquarie Point roofed stadium.

items.title: Missing Melbourne teacher allegedly murdered by brother in India

children.content: Indian authorities allege Melbourne maths teacher Sunil Sharma was murdered by his brother and dumped in a canal. He had been in India to prepare a property for sale.

image.altText: Relatives of missing Melbourne man Sunil Sharma who are accused in relation to his disappearance and death

items.title: Mourners farewell gangster a day after gunman targets wrong funeral venue

children.content: Dozens of mourners have turned out to farewell Sydney crime boss Lorenzo Lemalu at a funeral held at Lakemba Mosque, in Sydney's western suburbs.

image.altText: A crowd of people dressed in black clothing surrounding a black and white funeral arbour and black hearse next to a lawn.

items.title: I loved my body, even when it was expected I wouldn't get to love another

children.content: I'd take a cure for the pain, but I wouldn't take a cure for my appearance. This is who I am and why should I change myself to make other people more comfortable?

items.title: Meet the man born, raised and married in his local town hall

children.content: Brian Baker, 95, spent the first 21 years of his life in living quarters at the York Town Hall where his father was caretaker.

image.altText: Man on mobility scooter dwarfed by large ornate stage and red velvet curtains

children.content: Six Australian filmmakers are about to embark on the journey of a lifetime on Race Around The World. Here's how they're preparing for the unknown.

items.title: Debunking Australian weather's made-up terms and pseudoscience

children.content: Meteorological rhetoric in Australia, including from the media, can at times be inaccurate, misconstrued or just completely fictitious.

items.title: Commuters call for action after machete brawl at Flinders Street Station

children.content: Police say up to 10 people were fighting inside the station on Saturday night before some boarded a train still armed with machetes.

items.title: Homelessness service concerned by changing demographic in clients

children.content: As cost of living and housing pressures intensify, a South Australian homelessness service says more young women and single mothers are reaching out for support.

items.title: Private medical imaging provider bills Medicare for non-eligible services

children.content: A private medical imaging provider operating in a public hospital has been billing Medicare for some services that are not eligible for bulk billing, according to an internal memo obtained by the ABC.

image.altText: CUSTOM 340x180 Medicare card coins generic

items.title: Trump battles historians for ownership of US presidential records

children.content: The Trump administration is pushing for a return to pre-Nixon days when US presidents could do whatever they wanted with presidential records.

image.altText: Donald Trump holds up a pile of papers titled 'Accomplishments'.

items.title: 'Cybertongue' could prevent 70 million tonnes of milk waste every year

children.content: About a sixth of the world's milk is wasted, over 150 million tonnes every year, but a Canberra company has come up with new technology that could drastically reduce that.

Milk being processed in a metal vat in a warehouse.
A sleek white machine with the word "cybertongue" printed on it and small plastic pipettes next to it.

items.title: 'Right' decision: Lakemba Nights stallholders save thousands under changes

children.content: Considered as Australia's largest cultural festival, the Lakemba Nights food markets has been attracting more people every year amid the event's rising costs.

image.altText: Lakemba Nights stallholder Mohammed Zarqa

items.title: As vaccination rates fall in Australia, a British mother has a warning

children.content: Measles killed Rebecca Archer's daughter Renae slowly and silently over a decade. She has a message for the growing number of parents hesitant to have their children vaccinated.

image.altText: A woman with a serious expression holds a picture of a young girl who is smiling.

items.title: All it took was a joke for a small town priest to meet the Pope in Vatican City

items.title: 'Almost dying wasn't on my bingo card': Biles reveals 'scariest' health scare

items.title: Fed-up locals get hands dirty to restore pride in Tablelands town

children.content: Atherton was once named Queensland's tidiest town, but some residents say the main street has become unsightly. Locals have formed a volunteer clean-up crew to restore the Tablelands town to its former glory.

items.title: Palestinian baby killed by Israeli gunfire in occupied West Bank, health ministry says

children.content: Israeli troops shot at a car in the occupied West Bank, killing a seven-month-old baby and wounding his parents, the Palestinian health ministry says.

items.title: Family of coopers keeping the artisan trade of making barrels alive

children.content: Master cooper Dave Schmeider spent nearly 56 years in the barrel-making trade and reckons his "nose is stuffed" and his body is telling him to "back up"; luckily, daughter, Mell, has followed him into the business.
